name: microsoft-testing
description: An extension to create and run load tests using Azure Load Testing.

commands.title: Load Testing: Run load test (Azure Load Testing)
Load Testing: View load test runs

commands.name: setupLoadTestSecretsInAzure
commands.description: Setup Load Test Secrets in Azure Key Vault

commands.name: runLoadTestInAzure
commands.description: Run the load test in Azure

microsoft-testing.selectedSubscriptions.description: List of selected subscriptions for Azure Load Testing.

walkthroughs.title: Get Started with Load Testing
walkthroughs.description: Easily create a Locust or JMeter load tests using Copilot, iterate locally, and scale effortlessly in Azure.

steps.title: Create a load Test
steps.description:
Generate a load script by selecting a Postman collection or an HTTP file. Use Copilot to enhance your test with multiple operations, authentication, and best practices.

[Create a Load Test](command:testing.load.copilot.createLoadTest)

media.altText: Illustration of the Create Load Test command in action.

steps.title: Refine and Run Locally
steps.description:
Iteratively refine your script with Copilot and validate it quickly by running the test locally.

[Run Locally](command:testing.load.runLoadTestLocally)

media.altText: Example of running a load test locally.

steps.title: Scale with Azure Load Testing
steps.description:
Run your test in Azure to scale seamlessly and analyze rich test reports.

[Run in Azure](command:testing.load.runLoadTestInAzure)

media.altText: Demonstration of running a load test in Azure.

languageModelTools.name: create_load_test_script
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Generates a load test script based on Locust or JMeter from a URL endpoint or an input file (Postman, Insomnia, or HTTP file). If the user does not provide required parameters (such as endpoint_url or input_file_path), the tool will interactively prompt the user for the missing information during execution, so still you can call this tool to generate script. Use this tool to quickly create a Locust script for load testing.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Generate a load test script based on Locust or JMeter using a URL endpoint or an input file (Postman, Insomnia, or HTTP file). If you do not provide all required details, you can still execute this tool.

endpoint_url.description: The target URL endpoint to load test (e.g., https://example.com). If not provided, you can still call this tool to generate a script.

input_file_path.description: The absolute path to the input file (Postman, Insomnia, or HTTP file) for REST APIs. If not provided, you can still call this tool to generate a script.

virtual_user_count.description: The number of virtual users to simulate (e.g., 50). If not provided, you can still call this tool to generate a script.

test_duration_seconds.description: The duration of the test in seconds (e.g., 120). If not provided, you can still call this tool to generate a script.

input_file_type.description: The type of input file (postman, insomnia, http). If not provided, you can still call this tool to generate a script.

testType.description: The type of test to generate (Locust, JMeter). If not provided, you can still call this tool to generate a script.

languageModelTools.name: select_azure_load_testing_resource
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Selects and returns the Azure Load Testing resource ID. If the user does not specify a resource, the tool will prompt the user to select one from available resources during execution. Use this tool to choose a resource for test execution and analysis.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Select an Azure Load Testing resource to use for test execution and analysis. If you do not specify a resource, you will be prompted to select one.

languageModelTools.name: run_load_test_in_azure
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Run a load test using Azure Load Testing. If a load test configuration file exists in the repository, it should be favored over providing a script path or manual parameters. If there is no configuration file, then a test script path needs to be provided. If there are no test scripts, then use the create_load_test_script tool to generate a script.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Run a load test in Azure Load Testing using a script or configuration file. If you do not provide all required details, you can still call this tool.

script_file_path.description: The absolute path to the Locust or JMeter load test script to run in Azure Load Testing. If not provided, you can still call this tool.

azure_load_testing_resourceId.description: The full Azure Load Testing resource ID (e.g., /subscriptions/000.../resourceGroups/myResourceGroup/providers/Microsoft.LoadTestService/loadTestResources/myLoadTestResource). If not provided, you can use tool select_azure_load_testing_resource to select a resource.

virtual_user_count.description: The number of virtual users to simulate (e.g., 50). If not provided, you can still call this tool.

test_duration_seconds.description: The duration of the test in seconds (e.g., 120). If not provided, you can still call this tool.

configuration_file_path.description: The absolute path to the load test configuration file to run in Azure Load Testing. If not provided, you can still call this tool.

languageModelTools.name: select_azure_load_test_run
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Selects a specific test run from an Azure Load Testing resource. If the user does not specify a resource or test run, the tool will prompt the user to select from available options during execution. Use this tool to choose a test run for further analysis.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Choose a specific load test run within an Azure Load Testing resource. If you do not specify a resource or test run, you will be prompted to select one.

azure_load_testing_resourceId.description: The full Azure Load Testing resource ID to get insights from. If not provided, you can still call this tool to generate a script.

languageModelTools.name: get_azure_load_test_run_insights
languageModelTools.displayName: Get Azure Load Test Run Insights
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Retrieves performance and diagnostic insights from a specific Azure Load Testing run using its test run ID and resource ID. If any required parameters are missing, the tool will prompt the user to provide them during execution. Use this tool to analyze test results.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Retrieve insights from a specific Azure Load Testing run. If you do not provide all required details, you will be prompted for them.

test_run_id.description: The ID of the load test run to fetch insights for. If not provided, you can still call this tool to generate a script.

azure_load_testing_resourceId.description: The full Azure Load Testing resource ID to get insights from. If not provided, you can still call this tool to generate a script.

scripts.test: npm run build && npm run compile-tests && vscode-test --user-data-dir=/tmp --install-extension github.copilot
